name: SpacetimeDB Domain Module
summary: Structure a SpacetimeDB Rust module as pure domain slices mirrored by SpacetimeDB-aware table, repository, mapper, reducer, and view slices.

items: Put pure business rules in `domain/<slice>/` when a slice has invariants, lifecycle, policy, or cross-aggregate decisions.
Put SpacetimeDB-facing code in `spacetime/<slice>/`: table rows, input DTOs, mappers, repositories, reducers, and slice-local reconciliation.
Reducers authorize, load through repositories, call domain behavior, save through repositories, and record platform side effects such as action logs or events.
Tables own persisted and synced state; repositories own indexed table access and storage identity details.
Views expose caller-scoped read projections and should not become mutation or domain-rule entrypoints.

items.text: Every rich domain slice reachable from clients has a matching `spacetime/<slice>/` boundary.
Platform-only concerns such as authz, identity, views, sync helpers, and action logs may live only under `spacetime/`.
Cross-slice reducers live with the domain service or platform operation they coordinate, not hidden in an unrelated aggregate.

blocks.reason: The architecture depends on explicit domain and platform boundaries.
